title: Gratuitous Vec<u8> clone in media upload before spawn_blocking
description: media.rs line 195: `let data = data.clone();` clones the entire uploaded file buffer (potentially megabytes) to move it into spawn_blocking. The original `data` is never used again after this point (it's consumed only inside the closure). What goes wrong: doubles peak memory usage per upload — a 40MB upload temporarily holds 80MB. The fix is to move the owned `data` directly into the closure instead of cloning it (remove the shadow binding and use `move` on the original).
